During fiscal years 2010, 2011, 2012, and 2013, the Secretary may maintain up to 32,000,000 acres in the conservation reserve at any 1 time.”Pub. Regulations, , , 1549A–78, required the Secretary of Agriculture, as soon as practicable after , to promulgate regulations to implement the amendments by section 1(a) [title XI] of , amending former sections 3831 and 3832 of this title.Pub. L. 106–387, § 1(a) [title XI, § 1105]114 Stat. 1549Pub. L. 106–387Oct. 28, 2000Oct. 28, 2000

Eligible Land; State Law Requirements, , , provided that:Pub. L. 115–334, title II, § 2209132 Stat. 4551Dec. 20, 2018“The Secretary shall revise paragraph (4) of section 1410.6(d) of title 7, Code of Federal Regulations, to provide that land enrolled under a Conservation Reserve Enhancement Program agreement initially established before (including an amended or successor Conservation Reserve Enhancement Program agreement, as determined by the Secretary), shall not be ineligible for enrollment in the conservation reserve program established under subchapter B of chapter 1 of subtitle D of title XII of the Food Security Act of 1985 ( et seq.) under that paragraph if the Deputy Administrator (as defined in section 1410.2(b) of title 7, Code of Federal Regulations (or successor regulations)), on recommendation from and in consultation with the applicable State technical committee established under section 1261(a) of the Food Security Act of 1985 () determines, under such terms and conditions as the Deputy Administrator, in consultation with the State technical committee, determines to be appropriate, that making that land eligible for enrollment in that program is not contrary to the purposes of that program.”January 1, 201416 U.S.C. 383116 U.S.C. 3861(a)

Effect on Existing Contracts, , , provided that:Pub. L. 113–79, title II, § 2008128 Stat. 720Feb. 7, 2014“(a).—In GeneralExcept as provided in paragraph (2), the amendments made by this subtitle [subtitle A (§§ 2001–2008) of title II of , amending this section and sections 3831b and 3832 to 3835 of this title and repealing ] shall not affect the validity or terms of any contract entered into by the Secretary of Agriculture under subchapter B of chapter 1 of subtitle D of title XII of the Food Security Act of 1985 ( et seq.) before the date of enactment of the Agricultural Act of 2014 [], or any payments required to be made in connection with the contract.Pub. L. 113–79section 3835a of this title16 U.S.C. 3831Feb. 7, 2014“(b).—Updating of Existing ContractsThe Secretary shall permit an owner or operator of land subject to a contract entered into under subchapter B of chapter 1 of subtitle D of title XII of the Food Security Act of 1985 ( et seq.) before the date of enactment of the Agricultural Act of 2014 [], to update the contract to reflect the activities and uses of land under contract permitted under the terms and conditions of section 1233(b) of that Act [] (as amended by section 2004), as determined appropriate by the Secretary.”16 U.S.C. 383116 U.S.C. 3833(b)Feb. 7, 2014

Study on Economic Effects, , , provided that, not later than 18 months after , the Secretary of Agriculture would submit to Congress a report, to include specified analyses, that describes the economic and social effects on rural communities resulting from the conservation reserve program established under subchapter B of chapter 1 of subtitle D of title XII of the Food Security Act of 1985 ( et seq.).Pub. L. 107–171, title II, § 2101(b)116 Stat. 25216 U.S.C. 3831May 13, 2002May 13, 2002

Study of Impact of Pilot Program, , , 1549A–78, required the Secretary of Agriculture to conduct a study of the impact of the pilot program established under former , as added by section 1(a) [title XI, § 1102(a)] of , and to report on the results of the study to committees of Congress not later than .Pub. L. 106–387, § 1(a) [title XI, § 1104]114 Stat. 1549section 3831(h) of this titlePub. L. 106–387Oct. 28, 2000Mar. 1, 2003

Study of Land Use for Expiring Contracts and Extension of Authority, , , which required the Secretary of Agriculture to conduct a study of cropland subject to expiring conservation reserve contracts entered into prior to , and to report on the study to committees of Congress not later than , and authorized the Secretary, during calendar years 1996 to 2000, to extend up to 10 years contracts entered into under this subpart prior to , or to purchase long-term or permanent easements as provided for in former part III of this subchapter, at the option of the owner or operator on land that the Secretary has determined under the study should remain in conserving uses, was repealed by , , .Pub. children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: Through the 2023 fiscal year, the Secretary shall formulate and carry out a conservation reserve program under which land is enrolled through the use of contracts to assist owners and operators of land specified in subsection (b) to conserve and improve the soil, water, and wildlife resources of such land and to address issues raised by State, regional, and national conservation initiatives.

children.label: i
children.text: if permitted to remain untreated could substantially reduce the agricultural production capability for future generations; or

children.label: ii
children.text: cannot be farmed in accordance with a plan that complies with the requirements of subchapter II; and

children.label: B
children.text: the Secretary determines had a cropping history or was considered to be planted for 4 of the 6 years preceding , on the condition that the Secretary shall consider to be planted cropland enrolled in the conservation reserve program;December 20, 2018

marginal pasture land to be devoted to appropriate vegetation, including trees, in or near riparian areas, or devoted to similar water quality purposes (including marginal pastureland converted to wetland or established as wildlife habitat);

children.label: A

children.label: B

children.label: C
children.text: could provide habitat for animal and plant populations of significant ecological value if the land is retained in its current use or restored to a natural condition;

children.label: I

children.label: i
children.text: contribute to the degradation of soil, water, or air quality; or

children.label: ii
children.text: pose an on-site or off-site environmental threat to soil, water, or air quality;

children.label: i
children.text: newly-created, permanent grass sod waterway; or

children.label: ii

children.label: C
children.text: the land will be devoted to newly established living snow fences, permanent wildlife habitat, windbreaks, shelterbelts, salt tolerant vegetation, field borders, or practices to benefit State or federally identified wellhead protection areas;

children.label: D
children.text: the land poses an off-farm environmental threat, or a threat of continued degradation of productivity due to soil salinity, if permitted to remain in production; or

children.label: E
children.text: enrollment of the land would facilitate a net savings in groundwater or surface water resources of the agricultural operation of the producer;

children.label: A
children.text: more than 50 percent of the land in the field is enrolled as a buffer or filterstrip, or more than 75 percent of the land in the field is enrolled as a conservation practice other than as a buffer or filterstrip; and

children.label: i
children.text: infeasible to farm; and

children.label: ii
children.text: enrolled at regular rental rates; or

children.label: A
children.text: that was enrolled in the conservation reserve program under a 15-year contract that expired on , or ;September 30, 2017September 30, 2018

children.label: B
children.text: for which there was no opportunity for additional enrollment in that program; and

children.label: C
children.text: on which the conservation practice under the expired contract under subparagraph (A) is maintained.

children.heading: Planting status of certain land
children.label: c Planting status of certain land
children.text: For purposes of determining the eligibility of land to be placed in the conservation reserve established under this subpart, land shall be considered to be planted to an agricultural commodity during a crop year if, during the crop year, the land was devoted to a conserving use.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: In enrolling acres under subparagraph (A), the Secretary shall make the program available to owners or operators of eligible land on an annual enrollment basis with one or more ranking periods.

children.heading: Timing of grassland ranking period
children.label: ii Timing of grassland ranking period
children.text: For purposes of grasslands described in subsection (b)(3), the Secretary shall announce at least 1 ranking period subsequent to the announcement of general enrollment offers.

children.label: i
children.text: the Secretary shall reserve the remaining number of acres for that fiscal year for the enrollment of land described in subsection (b)(3); and

children.label: ii
children.text: that number of acres shall not be available for the enrollment of any other type of eligible land.

children.heading: Water quality practices to foster clean lakes, estuaries, and rivers (clear initiative)
children.label: 3 Water quality practices to foster clean lakes, estuaries, and rivers (clear initiative)

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall give priority within continuous enrollment under paragraph (6) to the enrollment of land described in subsection (b)(4).

children.heading: Sediment and nutrient loadings
children.label: B Sediment and nutrient loadings
children.text: In carrying out subparagraph (A), the Secretary shall give priority to the implementation of practices on land that, if enrolled, will help reduce sediment loadings, nutrient loadings, and harmful algal blooms, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Of the acres maintained in the conservation reserve in accordance with paragraph (1), to the maximum extent practicable, not less than 40 percent of acres enrolled in the conservation reserve using continuous enrollment under paragraph (6) shall be of land described in subsection (b)(4).

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: ii Limitation
children.text: The acres described in clause (i) shall not include grasslands described in subsection (b)(3).

children.label: i
children.text: in the monthly publication of the Secretary describing conservation reserve program statistics, include a description of enrollments through the priority under this paragraph; and

children.label: I
children.text: new enrollments;

children.label: II
children.text: expirations;

children.label: III
children.text: geographic distribution; and

children.label: IV
children.text: estimated water quality benefits.

children.label: A
children.text: the average number of acres of all land enrolled in the conservation reserve in each State during each of fiscal years 2007 through 2016;

children.label: B
children.text: the average number of acres of all land enrolled in the conservation reserve nationally during each of fiscal years 2007 through 2016; and

children.label: C
children.text: the acres available for enrollment during each of fiscal years 2019 through 2023, excluding acres described in paragraph (2).

children.heading: Frequency
children.label: 5 Frequency
children.text: In carrying out this subpart, for contracts that are not available on a continuous enrollment basis, the Secretary shall hold a signup and enrollment not less often than once each year.

children.label: I
children.text: marginal pasture land described in subsection (b)(2);

children.label: II

children.label: ii

children.label: i
children.text: the Secretary shall, to the maximum extent practicable, enroll and maintain not fewer than 8,600,000 acres of land under subparagraph (A) by ; andSeptember 30, 2023

children.label: I
children.text: fiscal year 2019, 8,000,000 acres;

children.label: II
children.text: fiscal year 2020, 8,250,000 acres;

children.label: III

children.label: IV
children.text: fiscal years 2022 and 2023, 8,600,000 acres.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: For the purpose of carrying out this subpart, the Secretary shall enter into contracts of not less than 10, nor more than 15, years.

children.heading: Special rule for certain land
children.label: 2 Special rule for certain land
children.text: In the case of land devoted to hardwood trees, shelterbelts, windbreaks, or wildlife corridors under a contract entered into under this subpart, the owner or operator of the land may, within the limitations prescribed under paragraph (1), specify the duration of the contract.

children.heading: Designation
children.label: 1 Designation
children.text: On application by the appropriate State agency, the Secretary shall designate areas of special environmental sensitivity as conservation priority areas.

children.heading: Eligible areas
children.label: 2 Eligible areas
children.text: Areas eligible for designation under this subsection shall include areas with actual and significant adverse water quality or habitat impacts related to agricultural production activities.

children.heading: Expiration
children.label: 3 Expiration
children.text: Conservation priority area designation under this subsection shall expire after 5 years, subject to redesignation, except that the Secretary may withdraw an area’s designation if the Secretary finds that the area no longer contains actual and significant adverse water quality or habitat impacts related to agricultural production activities.

children.heading: Duty of Secretary
children.label: 4 Duty of Secretary
children.text: In carrying out this subsection, the Secretary shall attempt to maximize water quality and habitat benefits in the watersheds described in paragraph (1) by promoting a significant level of enrollment of land within the watersheds in the program under this subpart by whatever means the Secretary determines are appropriate and consistent with the purposes of this subpart.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: For purposes of this subpart, alfalfa and other multi-year grasses and legumes in a rotation practice, approved by the Secretary, shall be considered agricultural commodities.

children.heading: Cropping history
children.label: 2 Cropping history
children.text: Alfalfa, when grown as part of a rotation practice, as determined by the Secretary, is an agricultural commodity subject to the cropping history criteria under subsection (b)(1)(B) for the purpose of determining whether highly erodible cropland has been planted or considered planted for 4 of the 6 years referred to in such subsection.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: On the expiration of a contract entered into under this subpart, the land subject to the contract shall be eligible to be considered for reenrollment in the conservation reserve.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), land subject to a contract entered into under this subpart shall be eligible for only one reenrollment in the conservation reserve under paragraph (1) if the land is devoted to hardwood trees.

children.label: i
children.text: riparian forested buffers;

children.label: ii
children.text: forested wetlands enrolled under subsection (d)(3) or the conservation reserve enhancement program under ; andsection 3831a of this title

children.label: iii
children.text: shelterbelts.

children.heading: Balance of natural resource purposes
children.label: i Balance of natural resource purposes
children.text: In determining the acceptability of contract offers under this subpart, the Secretary shall ensure, to the maximum extent practicable, an equitable balance among the conservation purposes of soil erosion, water quality, and wildlife habitat.
